sqlite                    3070900         vc10_esss_dist_1  [vc10]  esss
vs2010_runtime            10.00.40219.1                 2    mirror
wheel                     0.29.0                   py27_0    mirror
zlib                      1.2.5           vc10_esss_dist_2  [vc10]  esss
```

Version `2.9.2+1` is a package released in our internal channel to test a new pytest release.

`conda-4.2.16` installs `pytest-3.0.5` as expected using both versions of the `environment.yml` file.

Here's my `conda info`:

```
λ conda info
Current conda install:

               platform : win-64
          conda version : 4.3.14
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.3.14
    conda-build version : 2.1.5

                          https://conda-channel/esss/noarch
                          https://conda-channel/mirror/win-64
                          https://conda-channel/mirror/noarch
                          https://conda-channel/mirror-conda-forge/win-64
                          https://conda-channel/mirror-conda-forge/noarch
            config file : E:\Miniconda\.condarc
           offline mode : False
             user-agent : conda/4.3.14 requests/2.13.0 CPython/2.7.9 Windows/8 Windows/6.2.9200

```


(Please note that the channels above can't be accessed from outside our company).

Changing `conda` versions, I can pinpoint that the behavior changed from version `4.3.4` (which installs the correct pytest) to `4.3.5` (installs the incorrect one).

It seems this was a bug introduced by accident.


cc @gqmelo @tadeu @prusse-martin

issue_title: Conda install should work with git repo (like pip install)
issue_labels: [{'id': 91066590, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/labels/1%20-%20Backlog/long%20term', 'name': '1 - Backlog/long term', 'color': 'FFFFFF', 'default': False}, {'id': 75607489, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/labels/Builder', 'name': 'Builder', 'color': '006b75', 'default': False}, {'id': 75608120, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/labels/Needs%20Decision', 'name': 'Needs Decision', 'color': 'c2e0c6', 'default': False}, {'id': 16544532, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/labels/type:feature', 'name': 'type:feature', 'color': 'e99695', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Conda install should be able to take a git, hg, subversion, or other repository.

It should: 
- run conda build on the repo URL (with no option to upload)
- install the resulting package

conda build URL should:
- download the directory
- if a recipe is found at the directory, then conda normal build is then run
- If a setup.py file is found in the top-level directory, then create a package from the setup.py file (see conda build setup.py)

-->

issue_title: "source activate /tmp/a2" fails
issue_body:
I was following the tutorial here: http://conda.pydata.org/docs/index.html
Here is my session:
```
certik@redhawk:/tmp$ conda create -p a2 numpy=1.6
Fetching package metadata: ..
Solving package specifications: .................
Package plan for installation in environment /tmp/a2:

The following packages will be downloaded:

    package                    |            build

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    numpy:    1.6.2-py27_4 (soft-link)

Proceed ([y]/n)? 

Fetching packages ...
numpy-1.6.2-py 100% |################################| Time: 0:00:00   7.49 MB/s
Extracting packages ...
[      COMPLETE      ] |##################################################| 100%
Linking packages ...
[      COMPLETE      ] |##################################################| 100%
#

# $ source activate /tmp/a2
#

So either I did something wrong, or the instructions that conda prints are inaccurate.

issue_title: Add "Portable" command line option to miniconda installer
issue_body:
We've tried to deploy a web application on Azure's web apps, using the silent install functionality of the miniconda installer. Since we're deploying to a sandbox there, we can't modify global machine state, such as the registry. We thought that we could install portably, but of course, among others, the installer registers the uninstaller, and this causes the deployment to fail. See https://github.com/projectkudu/kudu/issues/1862

I've tried to find the source for your .msi installer so I could open a PR, but I guess they're closed source as I couldn't find them. Can you add an option to the installer so miniconda installs 100% portable, e.g. without touching the registry, program files and environment variables? Alternatively, please open-source the installer so I can submit a PR?

issue_title: Issue in AbstractDxlIO._open() when using protocol 2, sync_read and not USB2AX
issue_body:
The code starting at line 133 in abstract_io.py tries to broadcast a ping. If there are Dynamixles that use protocol 2 (ex. XL-320) and they are interfaced using a different interface than USB2AX (ex. USB2Dynamixel) the code will fail after trying 500 times. This is due to the fact that (it's not very clear in the manual) with protocol 2 broadcast ping does not return a status packet. See this [link](http://support.robotis.com/en/product/actuator/dynamixel_pro/communication/instruction_status_packet.htm) - and scroll to the Ping instruction, point 3.
As a result creating an Dxl320IO with sync_read will issue an error:

`from pypot import dynamixel`

issue_title: With "delete_aliases: True" but no aliases present, "close" doesn't close
issue_body:
I love the "delete_aliases" flag in curator 4. 
However, people should be aware that closing the indexes will fail if `delete_aliases` is set to `true` and there is no alias to delete. This is due to elasticsearch's behaviour: 
`curl -XDELETE host:port/some_index/_alias/_all`
throws an error when there was no alias associated with the `some_index`. It looks like "all" means "one to many" here. I expected a "zero to many" interpretation and was surprised.
This error will cause the `close`  operation to fail. 
(This remains true if `continue_if_exception: False` is set, because this will not force the current action to be continued, but the subsequent actions, as explained in https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/4.0/option_continue.html)

issue_title: Docker setup suggestions
issue_body:
Here's an easier way to run it inside a container

```
$ docker pull azuresdk/azure-cli-python
$ alias az='docker run --rm -v ~/.azure2:/root/.azure azuresdk/azure-cli-python az'
$ az account login
...
```

this way 
1) you don't have to create a new container everytime 
2) you don't have to stay in the container's shell 
3) you don't have to login over and over again

I suggest documenting it like this.

issue_title: Wrong timeout description
issue_body:
___init__.py,_ methods: _receive_messages, receive_message_ you can find this text:

> :param timeout: The timeout in seconds after which to return None if no messages
>      are retrieved. If set to `0` (the default), the receiver will not timeout and
>      will continue to wait for messages until interrupted.

But it's actualy in millisecond as written in the _class ReceiveClient(AMQPClient)_ documentation from which are those methods created.

issue_title: ListGenerator
issue_body:
### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?

https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/blob/2709a0bb5b1647803fc25ee1292c6fc43782ed89/azure-storage-common/azure/storage/common/models.py#L53

### What problem was encountered?

The `ListGenerator` does not stop/reset cleanly. To be honest, I am not even sure that it is working as documented.

e.g.

`data_gen = table_service.query_entities(table_name)`

Then repeatedly calling....

`list(data_gen)`

... will give you a large list of data then on subsequent calls a never ending smaller block. 

Expectation. After the first call to `list(data_gen)` then either subsequent calls it is either reset and  the same data is returned or if you consider it exhausted then return [] or None  

### Have you found a mitigation/solution?

This is a guess. I do not know enough about the way this is being used. Otherwise, write a `ListGerator` for the purpose.

Try following the 

`__iter__` and `__next__` pattern where the `__iter__` sets/resets and `__next__` does the work.

Note: for table service, please post the issue here instead: https://github.com/Azure/azure-cosmosdb-python.

issue_title: aiohttp request exceptions can't be caught sometimes when encountering bad http message
issue_body:
## Long story short

When encountering bad http message, aiohttp request exceptions can't be caught sometimes.

For example, I use code like below to detect if a proxy is working:

```Python
import sys
import asyncio
import logging

import aiohttp


DETECT_WEBSITE = 'httpbin.org'


async def proxy_req(proxy_url):
    if proxy_url.startswith('https://'):
        protocol = 'https'
    else:
        protocol = 'http'
    detect_url = f'{protocol}://{DETECT_WEBSITE}/get'
    proxy_url = proxy_url.replace('https://', 'http://')
    print('Detect url:', detect_url)
    print('Proxy url:', proxy_url)
    try:
        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
            async with session.get(detect_url,
                                   proxy=proxy_url,
                                   headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'},
                                   timeout=10) as resp:
                text = await resp.text()
                print('Response text:')
                print(text)
    except Exception as exc:
        logging.error(exc)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    proxy_url = sys.argv[1]
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    loop.run_until_complete(proxy_req(proxy_url))
```

issue_title: Fix rendering of single quotes in pdfTeX output
issue_body:
Currently, when saving a notebook to PDF via LaTeX, any single quotes in notebook are rendered as unicode apostrophes.

- before: '
- after: ’  ([U+2019](http://www.fileformat.info/info/unicode/char/2019/index.htm)?)

This has a couple of problematic side-effects, particularly when rendering Python, etc. code output:

1. Code cannot be copied and pasted into a console
2. Scripts cannot be executed without specifying a unicode coding, even when the only apostrophes are in comment sections.

It seems like there should be an option at the pdfTeX level to specify that ascii characters should be used in place of the apostrophes (e.g., http://tex.stackexchange.com/questions/63345/how-to-make-a-real-apostrophe-or-single-quote-in-latex/63348#63348)

It would be great if this could be implemented in Jupyter's PDF generation.

See also: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/32973952/ipython-notebook-single-quotes-rendered-incorrectly-in-pdf-output

issue_title: coalesce errors while there are running jobs
issue_body:
This is close to working, though I need to rework the way exceptions get aggregated according to @olson-sean-k's suggestion: https://github.com/buildinspace/peru/tree/coalesce

There are two conflicting scenarios that we need to get right:

- When I have long running jobs, I don't want to wait until the very end to find out that there was an error. It would be nice to get some sort of feedback immediately.
- When there's nothing else to wait for, and peru is just going to print my error and exit, I don't want it to get printed twice.

Here's what we want to do:

- Gather all caught exceptions at the fork point, and reraise a container exception type that includes all of them in a list. Tests looking for a specific exception type will need to traverse that list, but will otherwise still work. It probably makes sense for nested gathers to flatten each other. For example, if there's an error inside a recursive module, we want to avoid something like `GatherError(GatherError(RealError))` by unpacking the inner gather.
- Have gather be a `PrintableError` that displays all of its contained error messages, and rely on the existing error printing in `main`. It would be nice if it was aware of verbosity somehow, for stack traces.
- Use the fancy display to show error information while jobs are still running. This will probably involve holding onto display handles after releasing job locks somehow. Maybe even add support for red text in the display...

issue_title: Android Studio allows to generate invalid x509 certificates
issue_body:
Hi!
Android Studio allows to generate invalid x509 certificates. The form allows you to specify a country name on any number of chars. RFC specifies that the country name must be a 2 character country code.

Androguard uses the cryptography library to parse x509 certificates which had signed the APK. If the country name of the certificate is invalid, the cryptography library raises this error:
```
ssl/x509.py", line 102, in issuer
    return _decode_x509_name(self._backend, issuer)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/hazmat/backends/openssl/decode_asn1.py", line 66, in _decode_x509_name
    attribute = _decode_x509_name_entry(backend, entry)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/hazmat/backends/openssl/decode_asn1.py", line 57, in _decode_x509_name_entry
    return x509.NameAttribute(x509.ObjectIdentifier(oid), value, type)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/x509/name.py", line 50, in __init__
    "Country name must be a 2 character country code"
ValueError: Country name must be a 2 character country code
```
The `cryptography` library issue https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/issues/3857


Plus, it seems that Androguard does not handle the [APK Signature Scheme v2 ](https://source.android.com/security/apksigning/v2).

issue_title: Please, make Let's Encrypt client less intrusive
issue_body:
I think we can agree on that reducing the attack surface should be a principle everyone should follow. That means install the least amount of packages that still lets you get things done. Now if I do a mere

    # letsencrypt-auto --help

It installs the following packages:

    python-devel python-virtualenv gcc libffi-devel openssl-devel redhat-rpm-config cpp dwz glibc-devel glibc-headers kernel-headers keyutils-libs-devel krb5-devel libcom_err-devel libmpc libselinux-devel libsepol-devel libverto-devel mpfr pcre-devel perl-srpm-macros zip zlib-devel

**without even asking**. `yum -y` all the way. If I do this as a sudoer _user_, it still becomes root in the blink of an eye, everything's run with `sudo`.

If you think it's acceptable to install compilers and a lot of libs and headers on each and every web server for... running a Python script, that's totally up to you. But please, at least ask us if we really want that.

Now, this wouldn't bother me this much if there was a way to set up a single, isolated VM which I could use to request certificates for all our servers. Except I, of course, can't do that. The script needs to be run on the server that'll serve those domains.

I personnaly erased all these packages and reverted to our Comodo certs for now and by the look of things, it'll stay that way. I want to make it clear that I'm not _complaing_ - it wouldn't even make any sense since you're giving away certs for free, which is awesome. This is merely a suggestion that you may or may not consider.

I do believe that most admins would greatly appreciate if letsencrypt came in the way of a single binary (IIRC there are tools for doing that in Python but FIXME) or a self-contained Python script (e.g. does serving HTTP requests really require kernel headers and a C compiler in Python? I think not) instead of a script that

a) installs a lot of bloat on web servers
b) sets up a Python virtual environment... somewhere, that may be trivial for you python guys but not for the average nginx admin and honestly, I really don't have the interest or desire to deal with it
c) installs packages via pip

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Validate recipients as list or dict
issue_body: We currently don't do any input validation for recipients. It'd be pretty simple to check for list or dict and throw an appropriate exception. This will help avoid some confusion around the error received when a string, for example, is passed.

issue_title: When is a branch considered "released"?
issue_body:
I see that I can choose my branch ("master", "0.9.4", etc.) on the main code page and click "Clone or download" and select "Download ZIP" to get a snapshot. I also see that 0.9.4 is the newest numbered branch here, and if I'm reading this correctly, the latest commit to it was almost two weeks ago, while "master" changes every day. Does that mean that 0.9.4 is frozen, and will not change? Is there any visible status that reflects that a branch is finished/released/frozen and ready for users to take and use with some expectation of stability beyond what we might expect in a development or beta branch?

This same question applies to librdkafka as well.

issue_title: How to change the default storage from S3 to localhost?
issue_body:
Hi,

I have been trying to figure out how to use django-ckeditor with amazon s3 or to adapt my code to support multiple storage backends. The RichTextField works but not with images since for S3 our links expire after a certain amount of time (1 hour). 

Our setup:
 - our default storage: `DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = 'storages.backends.s3boto.S3BotoStorage'`
 - as a security policy we have signed url and they expire after a certain amount of time. (we can not change this for the production bucket)

Since the links expires, the images that I upload within the RichTextField are also available only for a certain amount of time and then they can not be seen anymore. I thought of different ways but the simplest (not nicest) would be to just change the storage backend for the ckeditor field to a FileSystemStorage. 
This can be done on a simple django image/file field: `models.ImageField(storage=FileSystemStorage(location='photos'))`  as explained here http://stackoverflow.com/questions/10419248/django-storages-with-multiple-s3-buckets 

But I did not figure a way to pass it to the RichTextField and did not see it in the settings. Is there a way to do this or a better way to solve my problem?

Thanks,
Vlad

issue_title: Platform sometimes times out when retrieving data
issue_body:
When I call `dl.metadata.search()`, it's hit or miss in terms of whether or not the data are actually returned. Sometimes the search is successful, and oftentimes--for the same search--the platform times out.

For example, in the following code (which is similar to the provided example in the documentation), I'm looking for up to 200 Landsat images over Brazil from Jan 2016 - Jan 2017.

```python
from pprint import pprint
import descarteslabs as dl
import itertools

# Find matches for Brazil
matches = dl.places.find('brazil')
pprint(matches)

# Make it the area of interest
aoi = matches[0]

# Search for available imagery
sources = dl.metadata.sources()
groups = {}
for group, items in itertools.groupby(sources, key=lambda v: v['const_id']):
    groups[group] = list(items)
pprint(groups)

# Search over time/space/satellite 
swathlim = 200
date_start = '2016-01-01'
date_end = '2017-01-01'
const_id = ['L5','L7','L8','L8SR']
feature_collection = dl.metadata.search(const_id=const_id,start_time=date_start,
	end_time=date_end,limit=swathlim,place=aoi['slug'])

issue_title: Configuring only, exclude, load_only, and dump_only on fields.Nested()
issue_body:
ref https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/pull/572

There are currently a number of inconsistencies with configuration of `only`, `exclude`, `load_only`, and `dump_only` on `fields.Nested`.

The biggest one is that `only` and `exclude` are specified when constructing the `fields.Nested`, but `load_only` and `dump_only` come from dot-delimited fields on the `Schema`.

Additionally, the `only` parameter on `fields.Nested` takes a string, while `exclude` does not – and `only` and `exclude` on `Schema` both only take a string.

I would like to be able to use syntax like `fields.Nested(ChildSchema, dump_only=('child.id',))`. However, it's not clear to me how this would interact with the options passed in at schema construction time.

issue_title: Meta Class fields not inherited
issue_body:
Take two models that demonstrate inheritance:

    class BaseSchema(Schema):
        """Base serializer."""
        id = fields.Integer(dump_only=True)
        created_at = fields.DateTime(dump_only=True)

        class Meta:
            ordered = True

    class PersonSchema(BaseSchema):
        """Person serializer."""
        updated_at = fields.DateTime(dump_only=True)
        name = fields.String()
        email = fields.Email(required=True)

The `PersonSchema` schema inherits all the attributes of the `BaseSchema`, as expected. However, it does not seem to inherit the meta attributes (i.e. `ordered`). To ensure ordered output, it is necessary to include these two lines in every model:

    class Meta:
        ordered = True

I don't know if this is something that could be solved or not (I'm not all that familiar with the implementation of these 'Meta' classes).

issue_title: Bug in either FP conversion or addition
issue_body:
This is on the unstable branch.

The following benchmark is essentially asking Z3 to compute the addition of the following two numbers:

```
   15749125 / 64
   268632143 / 1024
```

represented as single-precision floating point numbers.

With `roundNearestTiesToEven`, these numbers in decimal are:

```
246080.08
16255.999
```

So, addition should produce 

```
262336.06
```

But it appears Z3 is producing a value that equals

```
2.1885552e-38
```

which is nowhere even close. I'm not sure if the bug is in the conversion of the constants, which I believe you recently modified, or in the actual `fp.add` routine.

Here's the benchmark:

```
; Automatically generated by SBV. Do not edit.
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FP)
; --- uninterpreted sorts ---
; --- literal constants ---
(define-fun s_2 () Bool false)
(define-fun s_1 () Bool true)
(define-fun s1 () RoundingMode roundNearestTiesToEven)
(define-fun s2 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven (/ 15749125 64)))
(define-fun s3 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven (/ 16646143 1024)))
; --- skolem constants ---
(declare-fun s0 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24))
; --- constant tables ---
; --- skolemized tables ---
; --- arrays ---
; --- uninterpreted constants ---
; --- user given axioms ---
; --- formula ---
(assert ; no quantifiers
   (let ((s4 (fp.add s1 s2 s3)))
   (let ((s5 (fp.eq s0 s4)))
   s5)))
(check-sat)
(get-value (s0))
``` 

Z3 responds:

```
sat
((s0 (fp #b0 #x01 #b11011100101000000000100)))
```

which is the internal representation for `2.1885552E-38`

issue_title: optimization results reverse
Special chars in connection strings cause issues
issue_body:
I stumbled across this when using a password with an @ sign in it with fs.smbfs.

The easy (short-sighted?) fix for this specific issue is to make the regex match greedy.

See commit here:

https://github.com/caesarshift/pyfilesystem2/commit/56b0a82a41e5f447c3bdcbc563e007d6f47339e2

However, I'm just now learning pyfilesystem2, so I don't know all the different string patterns that you allow for input. I don't believe the change from non-greedy to greedy will break any existing patterns, but I can't be 100% sure.

Additionally, the question then becomes, what if you want a ':' in the password? The partition method a few lines later would cause that to fail.

So maybe my commit is to short-sighted, and it is just better to support the RFC 3986 which says that special characters should be url encoded?

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6718471/escaping-username-characters-in-basic-auth-urls
http://www.faqs.org/rfcs/rfc3986.html

Thoughts welcome. I'm willing to submit a pull request with either (allow @ in password OR url decode username/password).

I suggest adding a way to deselect all cells. For example, pressing the escape key might be a way to do that. I also find the cleaner look without having anything selected would be nice for non-interactive presentations using nteract directly (i.e., without printing).

Additionally, it might make sense to automatically deselect everything during printing as well.

issue_labels: [{'id': 224899118, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyMjQ4OTkxMTg=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/bug', 'name': 'bug', 'color': 'f2bfa2', 'default': True}, {'id': 438489433, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw0Mzg0ODk0MzM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/courage', 'name': 'courage', 'color': 'eaeaea', 'default': False}, {'id': 224899121, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyMjQ4OTkxMjE=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': 'bbd35d', 'default': True}, {'id': 456650032, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw0NTY2NTAwMzI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/outputs', 'name': 'outputs', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 794373761, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3OTQzNzM3NjE=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/pkg:messaging', 'name': 'pkg:messaging', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 900078574, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5MDAwNzg1NzQ=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/nteract/nteract/labels/stale', 'name': 'stale', 'color': 'f9deac', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
I wrote a gist explaining the problem with streams that we're having. Opening this such that people more familiar with react and jupyter can help explain the problem to me! 

### Q: What is going on under the hood when executing + rendering an output?

From the nteract-front end we send an `EXECUTE_CELL` request. This request gets intercepted by the corresponding RxJS epic, `executeCellEpic`. Tracking down where code is rendered then leads us to `executeCellStream`. From this code we can see that we should investigate `handleFormattableMessages`, where we can see that 4 different jupyter kernel message types are handled by this function: `MessageType(['execute_result', 'display_data', 'stream', 'error'])`.

As we can see from the [messaging spec](http://jupyter-client.readthedocs.io/en/latest/messaging.html#messages-on-the-iopub-pub-sub-channel): streams come from data that would normally be sent to STDOUT or STDERR.  For very large streams, the amount of data being rendered is crashing nteract!

In order to investigate, we can open the current `master` branch of nteract. In one terminal we run `npm run build:main && npm run build:renderer:watch`. In a separate terminal, we run npm run spawn:debug to get a log of redux actions when the dev tools are open. 

From here, we can slowly start breaking nteract with a simple python `for` loop.

First:
```python
for i in range(50000):
     print(i)
```
After executing, opening the dev tools, and inspecting the `APPEND_OUTPUT` action, the logger should show a single append output action with an object:
```
Object
name:"stdout"
output_type:"stream"
text:"0↵1↵2↵3↵4↵5↵6...50000"
```

Lets investigate a second time!
```python
for i in range(80000);
  print(i)
```
Investigating the actions in this case should show two consecutive separate `APPEND_OUTPUT` actions. Each with about half of the data belonging to the final output.

In the case of:
```python
for i in range(1000000):
   print(i)
```
we get a bunch of the append output actions and the notebook is unusable for some time.
In the case of 10 million the app crashes upon attempting to render the data. We can see this by recording the process in the dev tools.

To investigate the issue further we need to look at the reducer for `APPEND_OUTPUT` and the `Output` component in `display-area/output`.

If we put a log in the Output component:
```
  switch (output.get('name')) {
        case 'stdout':
          console.log('rendering std out!!\n')
          return <Ansi>{text}</Ansi>;
```
We get to see whats happening in the case of large text files. The log of the react rendering is registering, but the actual act of rendering is not finishing before 
another APPEND_OUTPUT action comes in. This work keeps piling up, and none of the original views are getting shown. One would think that we should be able to get some performance gain by efficient diffing and merging of the VDOM from React. If we have someway of tracking that the rendering is done, and communicating that back to the executeCellEpic, I would think that we should be able to fix this problem. If anyone has any other ideas of what may be going on/ how to fix this, please help me out here!

C source code
```c
#include <pthread.h>

void* inc() {
    // int* j = (int*)i;
    // *j += 1;
}

int main(int argc, char** argv) {
    pthread_t thread;
    int rc = pthread_create(&thread, NULL, inc, NULL);

    rc = pthread_join(thread, NULL);

    return 0;
}

Test script

import angr

p = angr.Project('binary file')
s = p.factory.entry_state()
pg = p.factory.path_group(s)
while len(pg.active)  > 0:

print pg.errored

The CFG Creation works fine and consumes about 3 - 4 GB of RAM, everything fine. But as soon as i'm just iterating over the disassembly the RAM requirements explode. Let's get rid of the `read` permissions.

```

issue_title: Report and skip tests that fail due to bad exception handling
issue_body:
According to the [Exception Handling](https://pulp.readthedocs.org/en/latest/dev-guide/conventions/exceptions.html) section in Pulp's documentation:

> In the event of a failure (non-200 status code), the returned body will be a JSON document describing the error. […] The document will contain the following:
>
> * http_status
> * href
> * error_message
> * exception
> * traceback
> * error

Unfortunately, this isn't actually the case. One discrepancy is that an `_href` key is returned instead of an `href` key, as describee in [Pulp Issue #1310](https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1310). This means that the updated list of promised keys is as follows:

* _href
* error
* error_message
* exception
* http_status
* traceback

But this still doesn't match what Pulp actually does. Five tests are currently failing with messages like this:

    AssertionError: Items in the first set but not the second:
    'resource_id'
    'http_request_method'

And this:

    AssertionError: Items in the first set but not the second:
    'property_names'
    'http_request_method'

And this:

    AssertionError: Items in the first set but not the second:
    'http_request_method'
    Items in the second set but not the first:
    'error'

…et cetera.

Let's fix these failures by doing the following:

1. File a Pulp issue for each of these failures, either individually or as a group.
2. Skip the failing tests by referencing the bug(s) filed. For example, take this test from `pulp_smash.tests.platform.api_v2.test_login.LoginFailureTestCase`:

```python
def test_body(self):
    """Assert that the response is valid JSON and has correct keys."""
    self.assertEqual(frozenset(self.response.json().keys()), ERROR_KEYS)
```

And change it to this:

```python
def test_body(self):
    """Assert that the response is valid JSON and has correct keys."""
    if bug_is_untestable(12345):
        self.skipTest('https://pulp.plan.io/issues/12345')
    self.assertEqual(frozenset(self.response.json().keys()), ERROR_KEYS)
```

issue_title: Stable channel support for Gitlab
issue_body:
Hi!

At [packager.py](https://github.com/conan-io/conan-package-tools/blob/develop/conan/packager.py):470, we have:
```python
def _get_channel(self, default_channel, stable_channel)
```
This function filter the CI runner by environment variable, but Gitlab is not listed.  As result, **CONAN_STABLE_BRANCH_PATTERN** doesn't work at Gitlab Runner.

To support this new feature, you could use [Gitlab Variables](https://docs.gitlab.com/ce/ci/variables/).

I think that, this piece solves the case:
```python
gitlab = os.getenv("GITLAB_CI" , False)  # Mark that job is executed in GitLab CI environment
gitlab_branch = os.getenv("CI_BUILD_REF_NAME", False)  # The branch or tag name for which project is built
```

Saludos.

issue_title: Path cost calculation errors
issue_body:
During my work on #2775 I've been testing how path cost is being calculated, and I've noticed some oddities. Maybe it is me not understanding the fine details of the Thread spec, but more likely this is some bug. Here it goes:

First, I've been testing 'long line' kind of topology: devices are in line, each device can see only 1 neighbour each side, end ones can see only 1 neighbour. Pretty much identical to #2782. Each link was having RSSI set to `-90`, which should correspond to route cost of `4` on each hop.

I have WIP implementation of 'long routes' mode, where I can form a line of >4 routers. For starting, I've been playing with 8 of them. When I collected all their path costs taken from `router table` output, the table was mostly OK, but with 2 strange behaviours:

from \ to | dc00 | 8c00 | bc00 | 5c00 | 3c00 | 7800 | b000 | 1000
--------- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ----
dc00 | 0 | *0* | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16 | 20 | 24
8c00 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16 | 20
bc00 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12 | 16
5c00 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 12
3c00 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4 | 8
7800 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4
b000 | 20 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8
1000 | 24 | 20 | 16 | 12 | 8 | 4 | *0* | 0

How to read a table: row header is source rloc16, column header is destination rloc16. IDs are arranged in topology order, so rloc `dc00` was at one end of the line, while `1000` was the other end. So, `route table` output for `dc00` shown that it thinks that the cost to `8c00` is `0`, whereas `8c00` thinks that its cost to `dc00` is `8`.

As you see following 2 problems are striking:
- typically each node think that the cost to closest neighbour is `8`, the cost to one after that is `4`, and then all other costs are too small by `4`. I.e. I would expect cost from `dc00` to `1000` to be `28` (7 hops) not `24`.
- cost from end nodes to their closest neighbours is `0` for some reason. It is probably related to the 'next hop' value from `router table` being `63`.

Is that how it is supposed to work? I've expected pretty much `0 - 4 - 8 - ... - 24 - 28` kind of output.

So, I suspected, that my PR had broken something. I did a clean checkout of current master, and re-tried with 4 nodes. Here is the table:

to \ from | dc00 | 8c00 | bc00 | 5c00
--------- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ----
dc00 | 0 | *0* | 4 | 8
8c00 | 8 | 0 | 8 | 4
bc00 | 4 | 8 | 0 | 8
5c00 | 8 | 4 | *0* | 0

Which is consistent with the larger table.

Interestingly, lack of `3c00` node changed cost from `5c00` to `bc00` from `8` to `0`.

Also, if the max cost is 16, I should be able to add 1 more node and see 12 in the table, right?

After a bit closer digging, I've also noticed that 'next hop' value get weirdly reported. On each node I did `router 0x5c00`, and node `bc00` said that the next hop is `8c00`, whereas it should be directly `5c00`. 

Another oddity is that the LQ In / LQ Out values in table, sometimes shown `1` instead of `0`. But, there was no `2` or `6` in the route cost table...

Also, I waited 5 real-time minutes (nodes were run with `20` time speedup factor) before issuing `router table` command, to make sure that the routing costs stabilized properly.

issue_title: Barcode validation
issue_body:
There is a list of allowed data and barcode sizes in the [epson documentation](https://reference.epson-biz.com/modules/ref_escpos/index.php?content_id=128). We should be validating data length and content. 

A short term solution would be to set up regexes similar to [escpos-php here](https://github.com/mike42/escpos-php/blob/0365deee8591ac5e80f748dce06bf0082ea59833/src/Mike42/Escpos/Printer.php#L201), but in the future we might want to see if there is an external barcode library to validate checksums.

issue_title: BGP multiple address families problem on IOS
issue_body:
On Cisco Catalyst 6800 (IOS 15.2(1)SY1a):

If my BGP configuration includes IPv4 unicast and multicast address family, this results in following error:

`FAILED! => {"changed": false, "msg": "[bgp_neighbors] cannot retrieve device data: "}`

If I add both afi address families I get around this limitation
```
--- orig/ios.py	2018-03-21 11:08:30.000000000 +0100
+++ ios.py	2018-03-21 11:20:55.000000000 +0100
@@ -1226,7 +1226,7 @@

         Currently no VRF support. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6.
         """
-        supported_afi = ['ipv4', 'ipv6']
+        supported_afi = ['ipv4 unicast', 'ipv6 unicast', 'ipv4 multicast', 'ipv6 multicast']

         bgp_neighbor_data = dict()
         bgp_neighbor_data['global'] = {}
@@ -1238,8 +1238,9 @@
         # get neighbor output from device
         neighbor_output = ''
         for afi in supported_afi:
-            cmd_bgp_neighbor = 'show bgp %s unicast neighbors' % afi
+            cmd_bgp_neighbor = 'show bgp %s neighbors' % afi
             neighbor_output += self._send_command(cmd_bgp_neighbor).strip()
+
             # trailing newline required for parsing
             neighbor_output += "\n"

@@ -1247,7 +1248,7 @@
         parse_summary = {
             'patterns': [
                 # For address family: IPv4 Unicast
-                {'regexp': re.compile(r'^For address family: (?P<afi>\S+) '),
+                {'regexp': re.compile(r'^For address family: (?P<afi>\S+\s\S+)'),
                  'record': False},
                 # Capture router_id and local_as values, e.g.:
                 # BGP router identifier 10.0.1.1, local AS number 65000
@@ -1322,7 +1323,7 @@
                  'record': False},
                 # Capture AFI and SAFI names, e.g.:
                 # For address family: IPv4 Unicast
-                {'regexp': re.compile(r'^\s+For address family: (?P<afi>\S+) '),
+                {'regexp': re.compile(r'^\s+For address family: (?P<afi>\S+\s\S+)'),
                  'record': False},
                 # Capture current sent and accepted prefixes, e.g.:
                 #     Prefixes Current:          637213       3142 (Consumes 377040 bytes)
```

issue_body:
The defined applied field declinations associated with the AARM code appear to be incorrect for positions 11-15.  

In pmag.py, def designAARM(npos):

INCORRECT line:
  Dec = [315., 225., 180., 135., 45., 90., 270.,
           270., 270., 90., 180., 180., 0., 0., 0.]

CORRECT line: 
    Dec = [315., 225., 180., 135., 45., 90., 270.,
           270., 270., 90., 0., 0., 0.,180., 180.]

issue_title: Delay in messages sent via publishGatewayEvent
issue_body:
I'm writing a Python3 script that is meant to send messages to WIoT after it completes certain steps in a method. The problem I have is that these messages are never sent in the order I generate them and instead are sent in one big batch once my overall method completes. I believe it has something to do with the way Python handles threads. I have tried a number of ways of solving this but no success.

I understand why it's been written in a non-blocking way but equally need a method to send a message now and only continue once sent (or advice on how I should write my code so it opens up to allow the messages to be sent).

issue_title: Distributed TensorFlow
issue_body:
Hi Aymeric,

First off I really love this API, and it came out at just the right time for me. I was looking at TF for an upcoming research project and was a bit intimidated, but now I'm able to whip up a really nice convnet super quick, so thanks!

Secondly, based on what I read, TFLearn abstracts away device placement, right? So if I just toss my code onto a machine with two GPUs it will automatically put things in place, or is that handled entirely by the TensorFlow back-end? (The section on their website about this was a tad confusing)

Finally, with the recent release of tf version 0.8, how reasonable (or possible) would it be to implement a further abstraction for specifying distributed training? In [their tutorial](https://www.tensorflow.org/versions/r0.8/how_tos/distributed/index.html#distributed-tensorflow), it seems you need to specify a device for each variable. it would be really awesome if I could just provide some minimal configuration information and train the same network on a ton of machines without changing too much code.

issue_title: Window on the page won't resize
issue_body:
If I set the width and height, the plotly window will stay in that size and cannot be resized, even though the outer window can be resized using drag. And another problem is the plot can't be zoom in when the data are appended.
```
import visdom
import numpy as np
import time

vis = visdom.Visdom()
assert vis.check_connection()

win = vis.line(
    X=np.array([0]),
    Y=np.array([0]),
    opts=dict(title='Random!', width=200, height=200)
)
for _ in range(5000):
    vis.line(
        X=np.array([_]),
        Y=np.array([_ * 2 + np.random.randint(100)]),
        win=win,
        update='append'
    )
    time.sleep(0.1)
```

issue_title: Avoid overwriting the version file distributed in the source release.
issue_body:
The source tarball of `0.10.1` comes with a release of a `version.py` file, which gets rewritten on subsequent calls to `setup.py`. This hurts the build reproducibility of the package. I overcame this by commenting line 377 of `setup.py` via a patch, but perhaps the design should be reconsidered for the next release.

Cheers,
Ghis

issue_title: Replace changelog content with hyperlink in long_description in setup.py
issue_body:
The exact effective date of a release cannot be known in advanced with certainty, and since the `CHANGELOG.rst` file goes through the review process, and includes the release date, it would be best if the `CHANGELOG` was merged after the release not before. This would allow us to accurately set the release date in the `CHANGELOG`.

As of now the content of the `CHANGELOG` is displayed on PyPI, and cannot be changed, without making a new release. This could be a never ending problem, hence the proposed approach.

issue_title: csvsql: Add --unique-index a,b to add unique index
issue_body:
It is wonderful that `--insert` exists and provides a quick way to have a table created and loaded with data!

But if the source CSV file is improved, tweaked, or regenerated, then the user is dead in the water. The table in the database is now out of sync with the CSV data, and there is no facility for ever updating it. There are at least two ways that csvkit could solve this:
- Provide a `--drop` option that would, in a single database transaction, drop the table, create the table, and reload it with the new data.
- The really fun solution would be an `--upsert COLUMN[,COLUMN...]` option that attempted to insert all of the rows, but for those that failed switched to attempting an update using the given columns as keys to search for and match.

issue_title: load no longer works when used inside onecmd_plus_hooks
issue_body:
As of 0.7.5 load uses cmdqueue, which means running load inside onecmd_plus_hooks no longer works as nothing processes the cmdqueue in that scenario. Ditto for any of the functions which call onecmd_plus_hooks like run().

If do_eos were to return self._STOP_AND_EXIT, and a self._cmdloop() were added back to the end of do_load(), it would work as it used to.

Not sure if such a change is acceptable and compatible with the intent of switching to use cmdqueue to begin with.

issue_title: certificate verify failed when REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE environment variable is set
issue_body:
When the `REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE` environment variable is set, pykube is unable to connect to a server:
```
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 601, in urlopen
    chunked=chunked)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 346, in _make_request
    self._validate_conn(conn)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 850, in _validate_conn
    conn.connect()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/connection.py", line 326, in connect
    ssl_context=context)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/util/ssl_.py", line 329, in ssl_wrap_socket
    return context.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname=server_hostname)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 407, in wrap_socket
    _context=self, _session=session)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 814, in __init__
    self.do_handshake()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 1068, in do_handshake
    self._sslobj.do_handshake()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/ssl.py", line 689, in do_handshake
    self._sslobj.do_handshake()
ssl.SSLError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:777)
```

It appears that this is because the `session` object of the `HTTPClient` doesn't play nice when REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE is set. This is apparently a known issue in `requests`, and it doesn't look like there's an ETA for a fix (they're pushing it off to v3.0.0):

https://github.com/requests/requests/issues/3829

I can confirm this is the case by passing the `verify` argument to the `get` method:

```python
>>> api.session.get('https://192.168.64.7:8443')
...
ssl.SSLError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:777)
>>> api.session.get('https://192.168.64.7:8443', verify=api.session.verify)
<Response [200]>
```

This might be able to be worked around for now by adding the `verify` argument to kwargs in `HTTPClient.get_kwargs`. I'll investigate further and open a PR if that's the case

issue_title: Expanding certificates with Apache or Nginx installers consistently crap out w/regards to rewrites. Bugs or lack of documentation?
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
Ubuntu Xenial 16.04

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
apt (certbot)

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

Re-running Certbot in order to expand certificates while using --installer apache or --installer nginx and using --redirect consistently blows up.

It seems to me that this should really "just work" and I am baffled why it isn't. Seeing that so many other hosting companies are using LetsEncrypt - and I assumed leveraged Certbot - I'd think this would be a very common workflow which was rock-solid at this point.

This leads me to think that A) either hosting companies in general are not using Certbot, or B) I am using Certbot wrong.

## To reproduce (Apache):

Generate certs and use --installer apache on a clean Virtualhost where Servername (test.domain.com) is defined in the vhost config:

`certbot --webroot-path /var/www/html --authenticator webroot --installer apache --expand --non-interactive --agree-tos --email=my@mail.com --domains test.domain.com --redirect`

Now point another domain to the server, update the http vhost config and add it as a ServerAlias, and re-run Certbot with near-identical cmd line:

`certbot --webroot-path /var/www/html --authenticator webroot --installer apache --expand --non-interactive --agree-tos --email=my@mail.com --domains myrealdomain.com,test.domain.com --redirect`

**Result**

Certbot will fail with errors relating to being unable to expand/update rewrite rules and state **Unable to install the certificate**

This to me is exceedingly bizarre. Certbot made the rewrite rules in the first place, and being unable to modify these is a catastrophe.

## To reproduce (Nginx):

Follow the exact same workflow as the above, of course defining the domains in the vhost config before runinng Certbot. 

**Result**

Certbot will now fail authenticating the domain using webroot as it gets a 404 not found. Why? Because it has in the first run added this to the Nginx config:

```
server {
    if ($host = test.domain.com) {
        return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
    } # managed by Certbot

        listen 80 default_server;
        listen [::]:80 default_server;
    return 404; # managed by Certbot
}
```
I can almost see in the crystal ball that you'll just say here: Stop using webroot auth you numbnut! Well ... I am a host, and I don't know beforehand whether users have proxied their domain through e.g. Cloudflare. In this scenario webroot is the only auth method that works. So if I want to script my way out of that ... Well.. That would entail me having to try and look up the domain and see if it points directly to the server IP or not.

Yes, that would be a solution, but in my world Certbot made this vhost config, so it should really be able to deal with this? Maybe I'm being unreasonable here.

## Shouldn't Certbot be able to handle ServerName/ServerAlias (Apache) and server_name (Nginx) ?

It seems to me that in an ideal world I would never have to touch the server config. Certbot should be able to figure out that I am defining a new set of domains for this server (or expanding on the old set) and set vhost config appropriately and handle rewrites gracefully.

I have of course tested this workflow where I haven't set ServerName/Alias - and then I of course get a failure as Certbot says that it really wants me to define my domains in my vhost config. So yeahh...

## All this assumes that this is something Certbot is designed to do. It seems to me it is not.

Is the recommended workflow, in fact, when you want to expand certficiates, and use --installer and --redirect, is to:

1. Execute Certbot rollback
2. Do your vhost updates (updating domain names)
3. Re-run Certbot

This is the only approach I have gotten to consistently work and not blow up due to Certbot choking on its own rewrite config.

## If this is something Certbot should be able to do, then:

1. You should really test for this. It's not a complicated workflow.
2. Maybe consider having Certbot handle domain name definitions in the vhost config files (yes, I know this is exceedingly complicated)

## At the very least document this properly and provide guidelines. There is no mention of how to properly modify domains/certs using --installer in the docs

issue_title: Allow third parties to update the counterparty transfer
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

To support offline nodes without closing the netting channels a monitoring third party is required, the third party will be responsible to call `updateTransfer` (or a equivalent function) on behalf of the offline counter party.

## Solution

Allow nodes that are not participants to call a function that has a similar behavior to `updateTransfer`.

## Tasklist

- [ ] A new message format for a node to inform third parties about it's known latest balance proofs.
- [x] A new netting channel function for a third party to update the transfer on behalf of the participant.
  - [x] ~Forbid the third-party from overwriting a participant provided transfer. (Note: This is not to protect against bad behaving participants, but to protect against bad behaving third-parties)~
- [ ] unit tests for this.

## Details

The concept of dispute and penalization were removed because:

- only the participants were allowed to update the netting channel by providing the *counter-party* message, this meant messages are always pristine.
- messages are monotonically increasing in value, this gives a strong interest for the participant to provide the latest know message from the counter-party.

With the introduction of third-parties we lose the property of always having pristine messages, but as long there is one well-behaved and up-to-date third party everything is okay. At the same hand we cannot add penalization to third-parties that provide an out-of-date message because the node could intentionally not update the service.

## Related

~Monitor Channels & Dispute #82~
~Allow third parties to close the channel #182~

issue_title: Use constant lock expiration for all hash-time-locks of the same transfer
issue_body:
```
In the first interation of Raiden unlock had to be called on a per
channel basis, and the operation was only allowed for a channel on the
closed state. Because the unlock was on a per channel basis, every
mediator had to use a lower expiration for the lock it sent in respect
to the lock it received.

The hash-time-lock expiration was always decrease by a configuration
variable named reveal timeout, the setting was in number of blocks, and
represented how many blocks were required by the node to learn about an
on-chain unlock on the payee channel, and call unlock on the payer
channel. The reveal timeout setting included how many blocks the node
requires to learn about a new confirmed block, for the payee unlock,
plus how many blocks were required to successfully mine two
transactions, the close and the unlock on the payer channel.

The above dynamics changed with the introduction of the secret registry.
With the secret registry the channel no longer needs to be closed in
order to call unlock, once the secret is registered this information can
be used by the channel smart contract to unlock the any hash-time-lock
on-chain. Additionally the secret registry allowed the lock for *all*
mediators to be unlocked at the same time, this difference turns out to
be crucial.

With the secret registry, the semantics of the reveal timeout changed
from the time to learn about a new block and to mine two trasanctions,
to just the time required to mine the secret registration transaction.

The secret registration happens if a node learns about the secret
off-chain while the hash-time-lock is in the danger zone, where the
danger zone is a block close enough to the lock's expiration in which it
may take too long for the secret to be registered in time, becaase of
the risk of losing the payment, the node must send a transaction to
register the secret on-chain.

The above means a node will send a secret register transaction
regardless of how many blocks are left for the expiration of lock, be it
unhappy case of a single block or the happy case of `reveal_timeouts`
blocks. The rationale is that a node wants to receive the payment, and
if the locks is nearing expiration it should try to register the secret
to secure the payment. (Note that the unhappy case can be improved, but
the race condition cannot be fixed)

As a consequence, if the lock expiration decreases through the route, as
it was done with the first iteration, the secret registration may happen
too late for hop which sent the transaction, but soon enough for
previous hops. This means that for a path like A-B-C, the node C can
learn about a secret at block `n` which is `5` apart from the lock's
expiration, and the transaction can be mined at block `n+6`, C will not
receive the payment but if the lock expiration for A-B was larger B can
receive the payment.

The fix is to make lock's expiration constant. The initiator is
responsible to choose a value that is sufficiently large to allow a
transfer to be routed through the network without expiring, and low
enough so that it's not reject by too many channels with a low
settlement timeout. Once the target receive the hash-time-lock it must
include the lock's expiration in the secret request, which in turn is
verified by the transfer initiator, where it's of interest of the
initiator to make sure the expiration was constant through out the path,
otherwise the target would be at risk of not receiving the transfer.
```

issue_title: SoS Task Status
issue_body:
SoS task can have many different status and we will need to define what to do with tasks in these status.

* **`pending`**: no `.status`, no `.res`.
* **`submitted`**: (new state to add?) pending in PBS queue. The frontend will need to differentiate this status and the `pending` status.
* **`running`**: with active `.status` file
* **`completed`**: with `.res` file in `succ` status, all `output` files are available.
* **`completed-old`**: `completed` but the `.res` file is older than `.task` file, so the job might be rerun (e.g. with `-s force`).
* **`killed`**: killed by sos, by setting `.status` file to readonly. The pulse thread should then try to kill the task but we do not know if that would be successful.
* **`failed`**: with `.res` file in `failed` status.
* **`dead`**: without `.res` and with inactive `.status` file (no pulse)
* **`failed-missing-output`**: with `.res` file in `succ` status but `output` files are missing. 
* **`failed-old-missing-output`**: `failed-missing-output` but `.res` file is older than `.task`.

(Partial list of issues),
* No PBS status (need to add)
* When to quit sos with `no-wait` is not very clearly defined.
* Need to kill running tasks before forcefully resubmit a new task?
* Behavior undefined if we re-evaluate a cell with existing tasks.

@WilliamHPNielsen @AdriaanRol @jenshnielsen

issue_title: Python 3.7 code break (async becomes a keyword for Py3.7)
issue_body:
On the forum, Mr. Cedric Chee (http://forums.fast.ai/t/cuda-syntax-error/20177/5) identified the problem I was having due to the python 3.7 instalation. 

In his words:
_According to Python deprecation plans, async will become a keyword in Python 3.7; it means it can’t be used as an argument name, as it’d be a SyntaxError.

The async keyword was renamed to non_blocking in PyTorch since version 0.4.0. Ref - PyTorch CUDA semantics documentation, PyTorch Pull Request making this change._

issue_title: Upload Comment Auto-attach
issue_body:
The method `Submission.upload_comment` doesn't act exactly how one might expect. It will upload the file, but not attach it.

According to the [Canvas documentation](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/submission_comments.html), this is technically correct:
> The final step of the file upload workflow will return the attachment data, including the new file id. The caller can then PUT the file_id to the submission API to attach it to a comment.

Since `canvasapi` is meant to make working with the API easier, it makes sense to add the functionality to automatically attach the file as part of the same call.

Since the file is attached to a comment, the method should include the option to pass a string to add as a comment.

issue_title: Defunct jupyter notebooks in jenkins tests.
issue_body:
It turns out this is fairly easy to reproduce.

If you build the Ray docker image with

```
cd ray
./build-docker.sh
```

Then start a container

```
docker run -t -i ray-project/deploy:latest /bin/bash
```

First check to see if there are any notebook processes.

```
ps aux | grep jupyter
```

Then run the following in Python.

```python
import subprocess
subprocess.Popen(['jupyter-notebook'])
```

This will fail with a message like

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/conda/bin/jupyter-notebook", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(notebook.notebookapp.main())
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jupyter_core/application.py", line 267, in launch_instance
    return super(JupyterApp, cls).launch_instance(argv=argv, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/traitlets/config/application.py", line 652, in launch_instance
    app.initialize(argv)
  File "<decorator-gen-7>", line 2, in initialize
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/traitlets/config/application.py", line 87, in catch_config_error
    return method(app, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/notebook/notebookapp.py", line 1071, in initialize
    self.init_webapp()
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/notebook/notebookapp.py", line 919, in init_webapp
    self.http_server.listen(port, self.ip)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tornado/tcpserver.py", line 126, in listen
    sockets = bind_sockets(port, address=address)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tornado/netutil.py", line 194, in bind_sockets
    sock.bind(sockaddr)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/socket.py", line 228, in meth
    return getattr(self._sock,name)(*args)
socket.error: [Errno 99] Cannot assign requested address
```

If you attach to the container in another terminal window (filling in the appropriate value of `CONTAINER_ID`), e.g.,

```
docker exec -t -i CONTAINER_ID /bin/bash
```

and check for jupyter notebooks, you'll see `ps aux | grep jupyter` prints something like

```
root        56  1.8  0.0      0     0 pts/0    Z+   23:36   0:00 [jupyter-noteboo] <defunct>
```

Exiting from the original python process will get rid of the defunct jupyter notebook.

It's possible that in our jenkins tests the python process that starts the jupyter notebook is not exiting properly or something.

cc @shaneknapp

issue_title: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING behaviour assumes existence of an 'id' field
issue_body:
Thanks for this package, it looks like it could be very useful!

I've hit an issue with the `DO NOTHING` handling -- it assumes that a field called `id` exists on the model, though this isn't always the case.

``` python
class Foo(PostgresModel):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, primary_key=True)
```

This results in a table with a primary key field called `user_id`,

It looks like the error stems from the way that the returning data is captured in the compilers' [`_rewrite_insert_nothing`](https://github.com/SectorLabs/django-postgres-extra/blob/master/psqlextra/compiler.py#L158) method which uses a literal `id` in the query construction.

<!-- 
Did you find a bug with something other than a command? Fill out the following:

#### What were you trying to do?

Attempting to mute

#### What were you expecting to happen?

To mute/

Unauthorised.

issue_title: Built in commands "addrole" and "removerole" bug
issue_body:
Please be sure to read through other issues as well to make sure what you are suggesting/reporting has not already
been suggested/reported

### Type:

- [ ] Bug

### Brief description of the problem
The problem is with the [p]addrole and [p]removerole, both don't default to the author if no user is listed. 
The cog was not added by me, one of the base cogs, "admin", to locate the problem.
### Expected behavior
When I do 
[p]addrole Member
I expect it to add the role to me, as it says in the description "If user is left blank it defaults to the author of the command." Same thing applies to [p]removerole as well.
### Actual behavior
What actually happens is an error:
`[21/02/2018 16:13] ERROR events on_command_error 165: Exception in command 'removerole'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python36-32\lib\site-packages\discord\ext\commands\core.py", line 62, in wrapped
    ret = yield from coro(*args, **kwargs)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python36-32\Lib\site-packages\redbot\cogs\admin\admin.py", line 160, in removerole
    await self._removerole(ctx, user, rolename)
  File "C:\Program Files (x86)\Python36-32\Lib\site-packages\redbot\cogs\admin\admin.py", line 121, in _removerole
    await member.remove_roles(role)
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'remove_roles'`
Meaning that you have to mention a user for it to work, meaning you have to mention yourself as well. 
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a role
2. Try to add the role through the command, [p]addrole <role-name>
3. Try to remove the role through the command, [p]removerole <role-name>
4. Both give errors.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'function' object has no attribute 'tostring'
issue_body:
Hi, 
Thanks for the opportunity to use this cool library 🙂 

So, everything is going great until the `.save_dicom()` step.  

```
client = DicomCleaner(output_folder='/output', deid=my_deid)
client.clean()
```
scrubbing happens: 
_Scrubbing /Users/me/dicoms/dicom.dcm._
Then,
`client.save_dicom()`
gives
```
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-77-35491d2123a0> in <module>()
----> 1 client.save_dicom()

/Users/me/anaconda3/envs/python_p27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/deid/dicom/pixels/clean.pyc in save_dicom(self, output_folder, image_type)
    179             dicom_name = self._get_clean_name(output_folder)
    180             dicom = read_file(self.dicom_file,force=True)
--> 181             dicom.PixelData = self.clean.tostring()
    182             dicom.save_as(dicom_name)
    183             return dicom_name

AttributeError: 'function' object has no attribute 'tostring'
```

It looks like the problem is with the `read_file` from pydicom...but I'm assuming the dicoms are readable since they were opened and read in the scrubbing step.  

To experiment, I tried to save as a png:  
`client.save_png()`
and got 
```
Error in callback <function post_execute at 0x111179668> (for post_execute):
UsageError: Invalid GUI request 'pdf', valid ones are:[None, 'osx', 'widget', 'qt5', 'qt', 'nbagg', 'gtk', 'qt4', 'gtk3', 'notebook', 'tk', 'ipympl', 'inline', 'asyncio', 'wx']
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
/Users/me/anaconda3/envs/python_p27/lib/python2.7/site-packages/matplotlib/pyplot.py in post_execute()
    146 
    147             def post_execute():
--> 148                 if matplotlib.is_interactive():
    149                     draw_all()
    150 

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'is_interactive'
```

issue_title: subprocess file descriptor issues: sr sanity & sr audit fail or outputs disappearing.
issue_body:
Python recommendations have changed multiple times. There was os.system, subprocess.Popen, subprocess.check_call, and then subprocess.run.  when sr is invoked it causes instances to be started, and then the instances may in turn invoke other processes, such as sr_audit.  We end up with command output disappearing, in some cases.  note this:  https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#fd-inheritance which has changed things as well.

when we had subprocess.PIPE in start_instance, sr sanity would abort when invoked with run_command(), so I changed that to None, but worried that on older < 3.4.X the PIPE might be necessary... on the older version does sr sanity still crash?  dunno.  When the change is made,
sr sanity runs, (because I can see it restarting crashed instances) but there is no output.
